title: French Onion Soup
excerpt: Ingredients 30 ml each of butter and olive oil a handful of sage leaves, not too finely chopped 6 large garlic cloves, finely chopped 3 smallish red onions, sliced into 1 cm wedges 3 smallish white onions, sliced into 1 cm wedges 4 shallots, sliced into 1 cm wedges (these are the long …
html:
Ingredients

  • 30 ml each of butter and olive oil
  • a handful of sage leaves, not too finely chopped
  • 6 large garlic cloves, finely chopped
  • 3 smallish red onions, sliced into 1 cm wedges
  • 3 smallish white onions, sliced into 1 cm wedges
  • 4 shallots, sliced into 1 cm wedges (these are the long banana-type shallots. if you can't find them, use another
  • two smallish white onions instead)
  • 300 g leeks, the white parts, well washed and sliced into 1 cm rings
  • 500 ml dry white wine
  • 1.2 litres beef, chicken or vegetable stock
  • salt, pepper and Worcestershire sauce to taste
  • 6 thick slices of French bread (baguette), broken into rough pieces
  • 1 large garlic clove, finely grated and mixed with 60 ml olive oil
  • 150 – 300 g nice ripe brie cheese
  • 65 ml finely chopped flat-leaf parsley
  • 65 ml extra sage, cut into fine ribbons
  • 30 ml lemon zest

Method

Heat the butter and oil in a large pot and stir-fry the handful of sage and garlic until fragrant, then add all the onions, shallots and leeks. Stir-fry on high until they start to colour and become deeply fragrant.

Add the wine and cook quickly without a lid until the wine has reduced substantially. Now add the stock and cook the soup for another 20 – 30 minutes until the onions are soft enough for your taste.

Meanwhile, toast the bread in the oven until golden brown, remove, drizzle with the garlic oil and set aside.

Once the soup has cooked enough, taste it. More salt? Pepper? Worcestershire sauce? A splash of wine? Once it tastes right, ladle the soup into oven-proof soup bowls standing on a baking tray.

Heat the oven grill to high and position the oven rack in the middle of the oven.

Arrange pieces of bread on the soup. Tear the cheese into pieces about the size of the bread and press it in among the bread. Sprinkle the parsley, lemon zest and sage ribbons on top.

Now put the baking tray with the soup in the oven and grill until the cheese has melted and the bread is browned here and there. Remove from the oven and serve immediately.

Serves 6 – 8.

A bit of work, but easy!
